---
title: "Guan-Fu Chou"
subtitle: "PhD Student, Higher Education"
description: "PhD student in Higher Education at the University of Michigan, researching financial aid policy, institutional pricing, and postsecondary-labor market alignment."
about:
  template: trestles
  image: profile.jpg
  image-shape: round
  image-width: 14em
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:gfchou@umich.edu
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/guanfuchou
    - icon: file-earmark-text
      text: CV
      href: cv.qmd
---

I am a PhD student at the [Center for the Study of Higher and Postsecondary Education](https://soe.umich.edu/areas-study/center-study-higher-and-postsecondary-education) (CSHPE) at the University of Michigan, where I work with [Dr. Jeremy Wright-Kim](https://soe.umich.edu/people/jeremy-wright-kim). I am also an incoming Predoctoral Fellow at the [Education Policy Initiative](https://edpolicy.umich.edu) (EPI) at the Gerald R. Ford School of Public Policy.

My research uses applied causal inference and large administrative datasets to study how institutional pricing decisions and financial aid structures shape postsecondary access, and how postsecondary credential production aligns with labor market opportunity. A common throughline across my work is the role of accurate signals: whether the prices students see, the credentials states produce, and the data institutions report give students and policymakers the information they need to make good decisions.

Before coming to Michigan, I earned an M.A. in Economics from National Chengchi University in Taipei, where my thesis used matching estimators to study returns to college selectivity and location. I previously worked as a research assistant at Taiwan's National Academy for Educational Research.

## Research Interests

- Higher education finance and financial aid policy
- Postsecondary--labor market alignment
- Applied causal inference and microeconometric methods
